page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.large is a memory-optimized virtual machine instance featuring 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor running at 2.5 GHz, this instance utilizes a shared CPU allocation model under the KVM hypervisor. The system architecture is x86_64, providing hardware-level support for AVX, AES cryptography, and FMA instructions, though it lacks AVX-512 capabilities. It does not include bundled local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and features a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks place the ecs.e4.large in the lower tiers of compute and memory efficiency. The instance scored in the bottom 10% for both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as PassMark CPU and memory evaluations. Memory bandwidth for large uncached blocks is limited, with read speeds at 14.95 GB/sec and write speeds at 11.46 GB/sec, both falling into the bottom 10% category. Redis SET operations reached 2.53 million operations per second, representing a weak tier performance.

Given the shared CPU allocation and low benchmark scores, this instance presents a resource-dense but compute-constrained profile. It offers a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core, making it qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads that do not require sustained, high-performance CPU throughput. Tradeoffs include the lack of local storage and low baseline network bandwidth. Appropriate workloads include lightweight database caching, basic web serving, and memory-intensive background tasks with low CPU demands.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.large is an x86_64 virtual server configured with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, running on a shared Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 processor at 2.5 GHz. Operating under the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators, and has a baseline network bandwidth of 0 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates limited processing and memory throughput, with stress-ng CPU performance, PassMark scores, and large-block memory bandwidth all registering in the bottom 10%. However, with 8.0 GB of memory per core, the instance provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio. This makes it a qualitatively cost-effective option for memory-intensive applications that tolerate shared CPU resources and lower compute speeds, such as lightweight caching, basic database operations, or low-traffic web serving.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.large is a KVM-based virtual server with 2 shared vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon E5-2682 v4 CPU. It is designed for memory-heavy, low-compute workloads.
meta_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.e4.large features 2 shared vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, offering a high memory-to-core ratio for lightweight workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU virtual server featuring a high memory-to-core ratio for memory-intensive workloads with modest processing requirements.
